_readme: APPROVED recipe: every knob pinned (a later generic-default change cannot restyle this look). starcomb resolution: CLI > this file > GENERIC.

notes.starless_denoise: off is the one non-generic pin, measured: the post-stretch vst (the self-flat class's adaptation) crushed 40-50% of this high-SNR target's bright-object chroma (petal R-G +41.7 -> +25.1, core -49%, blue reflection B-G -43%) and flattened the survivors into soft patches — the user-reported missing-red defect. off gate-PASSES with the ladder's best numbers (colour 2.0 / grad 1.4 / blotch 0.9 / rings 3.3) and restores the red (petal +50.1); gx KILLED (gate colour 11.0, sky tint). Full stage localization: git log (the defect commit).
notes.spcc: broadband RGB from a mono filter wheel — SPCC runs sensor-null on the composed stack (the generic default; grounding measured immaterial on the one chip with a database curve)
notes.solve: narrow 53-arcmin field at 0.68"/px (FOCALLEN 1150 mm): solve with the position hint + the cached index scales — solve_field.py --ra=270.6 --dec=-23.0 --radius-deg=10 --scales=4-9 (the derived set reaches scale 1; scales 1-3 are multi-GB index downloads; scale 4 carried the solution)
notes.ladders: measured on this set: chroma_core 0/2/4 (identical bright-object medians; gate colour 5/4/3), cull_pct 0/25/50 (NULL), black_point 0/4/8 (sky floor 15/11/7; faint dust emerges at 0), starless_denoise off/gx/vstpost (see starless_denoise note). Generic values kept everywhere the ladder showed no dataset-specific reason to move.

approved.judgment: results/judgment_m20_denoise_20260710 (candidate denoise-off vs vstpost control vs the data author's finish; full-frame lossless pairs)
approved.note: aura +19.5 recorded with --ack-aura-warn: the fixed-px shell annuli read star-halo zone at 0.68"/px (scale-awareness BACKLOG); no visible ghost aura at 1:1
